_comment: Human verdicts on ttn_rebroadcast multi-play merge candidates that are NOT merges — the same forces recording two genuinely different works. Recorded by candidate_id (the 8-hex hash shared with ttn_audit). Read back as a suppression filter; append an entry whenever a candidate is triaged and rejected.

rejected.reason: different acts of one opera-ballet — Act 2 vs Act 3, distinct pieces fused by high title-token overlap
titles: "La Caravane du Caire (opera-ballet in three acts): Act 3
La Caravane du Caire (opera-ballet in three acts): Act 2

rejected.reason: the four distinct concerti of The Four Seasons (RV 269/315/293/297) — titles without an RV ref fuse via Jaccard on shared 'Four Seasons' tokens; the season name (Spring/Summer/Autumn/Winter) is not a numbered locator or key, so neither guard fires
same Four Seasons false-positive pattern, on the 'rearranged' variants — also already in ttn_audit_decisions.json under the same composer
Dream Pantomime and Overture are distinct orchestral excerpts of Hänsel und Gretel, bridged via Jaccard through the 'Dream Pantomime - from Hansel and Gretel' middle title
titles: Dream Pantomime - from Hansel and Gretel

rejected.reason: movement-vs-whole-work fusion: 'Missa Sancto Job: Kyrie' is one movement of the mass, not the same as the complete 'Missa Sancto Job'; the colon-and-movement-name pattern is not recognised by the excerpt-locator guard

Asturias (No.5) and Cádiz (No.4) are distinct movements of Suite española Op.47, fused via shared 'Suite española Op 47' tokens — same movement-name-disagreement pattern as Vivaldi Four Seasons (Spring/Summer/Autumn/Winter). Named movements of a suite are not detectable by the no/op/book/key guards.

Dance of the Blessed Spirits and Dance of the Furies are distinct dances of Orphée et Euridice, fused via 'from Orphée et Euridice' tokens. Surgical reject: only the 12 cross-pair (Spirits↔Furies) candidate_ids are listed here — the 6 within-Spirits and 3 within-Furies pair_ids stay, so each sub-cluster collapses correctly into its own merge on the next regen. Same movement-name-disagreement pattern as Vivaldi/Albéniz.
titles: Dance of the Blessed Spirits - from 'Orphée et Euridice'
Dances of the Furies - from 'Orphée et Euridice'

rejected.reason: Gigues (No.1) and Iberia (No.2) are distinct movements of Images for Orchestra, fused via 'from Images for Orchestra' tokens. Same movement-name-disagreement pattern.

Prelude No 1 (E minor) and No 2 (E major) are distinct preludes of the 5 Preludes for guitar, bridged via the 'No.1 ... & No.2 ...' two-prelude selection title: it carries both members' numbers so it disagrees with neither under the numbered-locator guard (the multi-member-selection union-find pattern). Surgical reject: only the 5 selection↔member bridge candidate_ids are listed — the within-No.1 and within-No.2 pair_ids stay, so each prelude's variants still collapse into their own merge on regen.
titles: Prelude No 1 in E minor (from 5 preludes for guitar)
Prelude No 2 in E major (from 5 preludes for guitar)
Prelude for guitar No 2 in E major
Prelude for guitar No.2 in E major (from 5 preludes for guitar)
Prelude for guitar no.1 in E minor
Preludes for guitar No.1 in E minor & No.2 in E major

rejected.reason: Two Roses No 1 and No 2 (from Au sein de la nature) are distinct pieces, bridged via the 'no 1 and no 2' selection title carrying both numbers. Same multi-member-selection union-find pattern; all three titles are distinct works, so the cluster dissolves entirely on regen.
titles: Two Roses: No 1, from Au sein de la nature
Two Roses: No 2, from Au sein de la nature
Two Roses: no 1 and no 2 from Au sein de la nature
